title: The Interval (remote violence)

fields.vision: The Interval is a MuranoOS room handoff for studying remote violence as a structure of separation between perception, command, vulnerability, and consequence.
fields.mechanism: It routes remote-violence analysis through distance, mediation, command chain, target vulnerability, perception gap, moral injury, and institutional responsibility.
fields.built_components: Documented handoff or prototype; live status still to verify
fields.source_utilization: Peacebuilding, conflict transformation, trauma, memory, and repair. Use source library peace/conflict, dehumanization, aggression, and memory sources as Book III and peace-room scaffolding. Vision: Make repair and violence pages peace/conflict-first, trauma-literate, and institutionally precise. Mechanism: Extract escalation, memory, dehumanization, perpetration, aggression, attachment, reconciliation, and repair mechanisms. Plan: Close-read Book III, Peace, violence, trauma, and atrocity pages before status changes. Status: source library identified as candidate scholarly support; direct quotation requires exact passage review. Remaining gap: Needs sensitive passage-level review and counterexample review. Source pool: 64 deduplicated local title candidates; 77 catalogue support rows. Examples: The SAGE Handbook of Peace and Conflict Studies; Peace and Conflict in Core-Periphery Conflicts. Discovery, creativity, systems thinking, and project generation. Use source library creativity/innovation/network sources to professionalize the project-development account. Vision: Explain Nexus and Personal OS as project-development and discovery infrastructure, not miscellaneous personal tooling. Mechanism: Extract creativity, network, learning, imagination, leadership, and project-generation mechanisms. Plan: Rewrite Nexus and planning pages around upstream/downstream project movement. Status: source library identified as candidate scholarly support; direct quotation requires exact passage review. Remaining gap: Need exact project-method and creativity passages. Source pool: 151 deduplicated local title candidates; 154 catalogue support rows. Examples: Creativity Research; The Heritage, Creativity and Innovation Nexus. source library scholarly-source routing with author correction. Raw filenames often append Nik_Murano and are not treated as authorship...
fields.plan: How this briefing says the vision should be accomplished, translated into a public project route. 7-step workflow per README: (1) Read product charter. (2) Read screen specs with SVG wireframes. (3) Open prototype/index.html for concrete visual reference. (4) Use tokens as implementation starting points. (5) Use component bible + components.csv to scope frontend work. (6) Use data map + schemas to scope data and API work. (7) Use acceptance criteria as definition-of-done baseline. Wire or preserve the implementation files: Runnable HTML prototype (prototype/index.html) — local reference, no build step required. Only a couple of zips in this bundle ship working prototypes.
fields.status: Documented handoff or prototype; live status still to verify
fields.remaining_gaps: Promote the packet from documented handoff to live implementation only after the declared caveats are resolved: apply or wire the scaffold, run the stated validation or smoke-test sequence, and keep unresolved assumptions visible.
fields.detailed_source_vision: Design-and-engineering handoff for the MuranoOS room The Interval — a chamber for examining remote violence as a structure of separation: between perception and vulnerability, command and consequence, domestic normality and battlespace, official narrative and contested evidence. Not a military console, not a generic article page. Product thesis: "Authority remains overhead, consequence remains beneath, and the user works in the interval between them." This is why the room is named "The Interval."
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: , state the backend/frontend or packet components, and decide whether the handoff is ready for engineering, public explanation, or archival retention

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.what_it_is: The Interval is a MuranoOS room handoff for studying remote violence as a structure of separation between perception, command, vulnerability, and consequence.
public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: It routes remote-violence analysis through distance, mediation, command chain, target vulnerability, perception gap, moral injury, and institutional responsibility.
public_pitch.why_it_matters: It turns an abstract ethical problem into an inspectable analytical chamber for conflict, technology, perception, and accountability.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: rendered correction table.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.

researcher_process_map.summary: The Interval is a MuranoOS room handoff for studying remote violence as a structure of separation between perception, command, vulnerability, and consequence.
researcher_process_map.final_use_case: It turns an abstract ethical problem into an inspectable analytical chamber for conflict, technology, perception, and accountability.

instruments.downstream_use: How this briefing says the vision should be accomplished, translated into a public project route. 7-step workflow per README: (1) Read product charter. (2) Read screen specs with SVG wireframes. (3) Open prototype/index.html for concrete visual reference. (4).
instruments.proof: Peace and Conflict in Core-Periphery Conflicts. Discovery, creativity, systems thinking, and project generation. Use source library creativity/innovation/network sources to professionalize the project-development account. Vision: Explain Nexus and Personal OS.
instruments.status: Documented handoff or prototype; live status still to verify
instruments.gap: Promote the packet from documented handoff to live implementation only after the declared caveats are resolved: apply or wire the scaffold, run the stated validation or smoke-test sequence, and keep unresolved.
instruments.verification_check: Confirm `Core-Periphery Conflicts. Discovery` appears in the cited source context and that the public page explains what output, decision, or project function it supports.
